ModMetadata.Name: Power Grid Plus
ModMetadata.Author: SixFive7
ModMetadata.Description:
[h1]Power Grid Plus[/h1]
Reworks Stationeers wired power: priority-based transformer dispatch with automatic shedding, proportional load sharing, three cable tiers as separate transmission voltages, logic-transparent transformer / battery / dish bridges, and deterministic multiplayer cable-network ids.
A pure-patch mod: no new craftable devices, no asset bundle. The simulation half is derived from Sukasa's Re-Volt (MIT); the three-tier voltage backbone and the priority + shedding feature are new. Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames.
This is an early build, not yet play-tested. The simulation, the cable-tier rule and the device-tier rule are all in. Devices are free to place anywhere -- a misplaced device burns the cable it's hooked into on the next power tick (as long as the network has actual power flow). Placing the wrong-tier cable next to an existing device is blocked at the cursor with a "Wrong voltage" message. Burned cables show why they burned in their hover tooltip.
[h2]Features[/h2]
[h3]Reworked power simulation (from Re-Volt)[/h3] [list] [*]Electrical load is shared proportionally across every source on a network, and split proportionally across every load. [*]Cables burn out gradually and probabilistically when overloaded, based on a short rolling average of throughput, instead of instantly. Fuses still blow instantly and always blow before a cable does. [*]Recursive and looped power networks are allowed (the vanilla force-burn check is off by default; re-enable it in settings). [*]Stationary batteries are charge- and discharge-rate limited, with an optional charge-efficiency setting, and expose their rate limits as logic values. [*]Transformers no longer leak free power and charge their own quiescent draw upstream; they expose throughput as the Power Actual logic value. [*]Area Power Controllers no longer leak a small amount of power or slowly drain their battery when nothing downstream is drawing. [/list] Re-Volt's circuit breakers and Load Center are not included; this is the pure-simulation subset.
[h3]Three-tier transmission voltages (new)[/h3] [list] [*]Super-heavy cable never burns out -- it is the long-haul backbone. Normal and heavy cable keep their ratings. [*]Super-heavy cable costs twice as much to craft (configurable via the multiplier; 1.0 = vanilla). [*]The three cable tiers are three separate voltages. A cable network must be all one tier; a wrong-tier cable burns on the next power tick (only when the network actually has power flowing -- an idle off network destroys nothing). The only legal bridge between tiers is a transformer or an Area Power Controller (its input and output are separate networks). [*]Devices belong on the right tier: generators and stationary batteries on heavy, the high-draw machines (Carbon Sequester, the furnaces, Centrifuge, Recycler, Ice Crusher, Hydraulic Pipe Bender, Deep Miner, plus any you add in the config) on heavy or normal, super-heavy is cables-and-transformers only, and everything else on normal. A device on the wrong tier is allowed to place -- but when power flows, the cable next to it burns and the device becomes orphaned (never the device itself). Transformers, APCs and wireless power devices are exempt. [*]Placement UX: placing the wrong-tier cable next to an existing device shows a red ghost with a "Wrong voltage" tooltip; same for placing a wrong-tier cable next to an existing cable network of another tier. Device placement is never blocked at the cursor -- you find out by watching the cable burn. [*]Burned cables explain why: hover over a ruptured cable for "Burned: overloaded" / "Burned: wrong voltage -- adjacent [device]" / "Burned: wrong voltage -- bridging different tiers". [/list]
[h3]Transformer priority and load shedding (new)[/h3] [list] [*]Transformer throughput is hardcoded at OutputMaximum; the knob sets a writable Priority value instead (default 100, step 10 per screwdriver click or 1 with Alt). [*]Competing transformers on the same input network are served strictly by Priority: highest first, leftover to the next, demand-driven. [*]A transformer that cannot get its full share sheds for 10 s: contributes 0, on/off button pulses orange, hover shows "(Shedding)". Two-tick tolerance prevents single-frame spikes from tripping a lockout. Auto-clears. [*]New writable [b]Priority[/b] + read-only [b]Shedding[/b] logic values. Legacy [b]Setting[/b] writes redirect to [b]Priority[/b]. [*]Priority saves with the world and rides the multiplayer join handshake. Master toggle [b]Enable Transformer Shedding[/b] restores vanilla when off. [/list]
[h3]Logic passthrough (new)[/h3] [list] [*]Transformers, stationary batteries and Area Power Controllers split a power network in two; normally a logic reader on one side can't see the other side's devices. Turn on passthrough and the reader sees through the bridge, and through a whole chain of bridges, so devices several transformers away read as one logic network, with no extra data cable. Looped layouts are fine. Linked Microwave Power Transmitter and Receiver dishes bridge across their wireless link too. [*]A writable LogicPassthroughMode logic value (set from IC10 or a Logic Writer) controls it per device: 1 = transparent, 0 = vanilla. Defaults to transparent for batteries, dishes and the small transformer; opaque for other transformers. Saved with the world. [*]Each device family has a server-authoritative master toggle. [/list]
[h3]Settings[/h3] All settings are server-authoritative: in multiplayer the host's value controls gameplay for everyone. Highlights: [list] [*][b]Cable Burn Factor[/b] (default 1.0): scales the per-tick cable burn chance; 0.0 disables gradual burnout. [*][b]Enable Unlimited Super-Heavy Cables[/b] (default true), [b]Super-Heavy Cable Cost Multiplier[/b] (default 2.0). [*][b]Enable Voltage Tiers[/b] (default true), [b]Extra Heavy-Cable Devices[/b] (a comma-separated PrefabName list). [*][b]Enable Battery Limits[/b] / [b]Max Battery Charge Rate[/b] / [b]Max Battery Discharge Rate[/b] / [b]Battery Charge Efficiency[/b]. [*][b]Enable Transformer Exploit Mitigation[/b], [b]Enable APC Power Fix[/b]. [*][b]Enable Transformer / Battery / Power Transmitter Logic Passthrough[/b], [b]Enable APC Logic Passthrough[/b] (all default true). [*][b]Enable Transformer Shedding[/b] (default true): priority-based transformer dispatch and load shedding. [/list]
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Requires:[/b] [list] [*][url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/]BepInEx[/url] [*][url=https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad]StationeersLaunchPad[/url] [/list]
[b]All players[/b] on a server must have Power Grid Plus installed. Matching mod versions are enforced during the connection handshake automatically. [b]Dedicated servers[/b] need the same BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad + Power Grid Plus setup installed server-side.
Re-Volt itself swaps the power tick the same way this mod does, so running both is not supported.
[h2]Reporting Issues[/h2] If you run into a bug or something behaves unexpectedly, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/issues]GitHub[/url]. Please include the mod name in the title so reports can be triaged. Steam comment notifications don't always come through, so GitHub is the reliable way to make sure a report is seen.
[h2]Credits[/h2] [list] [*]Built on the power-simulation work in [url=https://github.com/sukasa/revolt]Re-Volt[/url] by Sukasa, used under the MIT License. The Re-Volt-derived portions retain their MIT notice; see the repository NOTICE. [/list]
[url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/tree/master/Mods/PowerGridPlus]Source Code on GitHub[/url]
[h2]License[/h2] Licensed under the [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/blob/master/LICENSE]Apache License 2.0[/url]. Attribution required; see the LICENSE and NOTICE files at the repository root.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog: v0.1.0: First build (work in progress) - Reworked cable-network power tick from Re-Volt: proportional load sharing, gradual probabilistic cable burnout on a rolling throughput average, NaN-power guard. - Stationary battery charge/discharge-rate limits, optional charge efficiency, and rate-limit logic values. - Transformer free-power exploit fix, quiescent-draw restore, and Power Actual logic value. - Area Power Controller power-leak / idle-drain fix. - Recursive and looped networks allowed by default. - Super-heavy cable never burns; the three cable tiers are separate transmission voltages (mixing them burns the lower-tier boundary cable and splits the network); devices belong on their tier (generators and batteries on heavy, high-draw machines on heavy or normal, super-heavy is cables-and-transformers only, everything else on normal) and a device on the wrong tier gets no power; super-heavy cable coil costs 2x to craft (configurable via the multiplier). - Reactive tier enforcement: a wrong-tier cable burns on the next power tick if the network has actual power flow (an idle or off network destroys nothing). Wrong-tier cable placement is blocked at the cursor with a "Wrong voltage" tooltip; device placement is never blocked. Burned cables explain why they burned in their hover tooltip. - Logic passthrough: a writable LogicPassthroughMode logic value (set via IC10 or a logic writer) makes power-network bridging devices logic-transparent, so a logic reader sees devices on the far side, transitively across a whole chain of bridged devices (cycle-safe, so looped networks are fine). Covers transformers, stationary batteries, linked power transmitter and receiver dishes, and Area Power Controllers. The per-device mode is server-authoritative, persists across save and load, and each device family has its own master server toggle. - Transformer priority and load shedding: transformer throughput is hardcoded at the per-prefab OutputMaximum rating; the knob now sets a writable Priority value (default 100, step 10 per click or 1 with Alt). When transformers compete on the same input network, allocation is strictly by Priority: highest gets first dibs, leftover goes to the next priority. A transformer that cannot get its full share from the input network sheds for 10 seconds (contributes 0, on/off button pulses orange, hover shows "Shedding: insufficient upstream supply"); auto-clears after the hold. Two-tick tolerance prevents single-frame spikes from tripping a lockout. New writable Priority and read-only Shedding logic values; Setting writes are transparently redirected to Priority so legacy IC10 scripts keep working. Per-transformer Priority is saved with the world and synced across multiplayer in the join handshake. Server master toggle Enable Transformer Shedding (default on) restores vanilla behaviour when off. - Deterministic network merge: when cable or structure networks merge, the survivor is chosen by lowest reference id so the host and all clients agree on the same network id (a multiplayer correctness fix).
ModMetadata.InGameDescription: Power Grid Plus Reworks Stationeers wired power: priority-based transformer dispatch with shedding, proportional load sharing, three cable tiers as separate voltages, logic-transparent bridges, and deterministic multiplayer cable-network ids. A pure-patch mod (no new prefabs). Simulation half from Re-Volt (MIT). Multiplayer-compatible. Early build. From Re-Volt: - Proportional load sharing; gradual rolling-average cable burnout; recursive networks; battery charge/discharge limits; transformer + APC exploit fixes. New: - Transformer Priority + shedding: knob sets Priority; throughput hardcoded at OutputMaximum. Highest priority served first; lower ones shed (orange flash + hover) for 10 s on shortfall. Writable Priority + read-only Shedding logic values; Setting writes redirect to Priority. - Super-heavy cable never burns; it is the long-haul backbone. - Three cable tiers are separate voltages; mixing them burns the lower-tier boundary cable. Bridge tiers with a transformer or APC. - Devices belong on their tier; a misplaced device burns its cable next powered tick. - Logic passthrough: writable LogicPassthroughMode makes transformers, batteries and dishes logic-transparent. Settings: Server-authoritative; the host's values control gameplay for everyone. Requires: BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad.
